sources.name: ACLED — Armed Conflict Location & Event Data

violation_types: Arbitrary arrest or detention of a Palestinian
Arson or damage to Palestinian cars or other property
Arson or damage to Palestinian farmland, crops, or irrigation systems
Arson or damage to Palestinian homes
Assault on a Palestinian using sticks or other blunt objects
Attack on or theft of Palestinian livestock
Construction or expansion of an Israeli outpost
Raid on a Palestinian village by Israeli security forces
Verbal or armed threat on Palestinians by Israeli security forces

incidents.n: Property destruction: On June 19 2019, Israeli Settlers razed Palestinian-owned land in the village of Madama south of Nablus. When Palestinians confronted the Settlers, they proceeded to set olive fields on fire.

Looting: on Tuesday, May 10, a group of Israeli settlers broke into the village of Aqraba, south of Nablus in the West Bank and stole three sheep belonging to a Palestinian shepherd. No causalities were reported.
On Wednesday May 25, an Israeli settler purposely ran over a Palestinian four-year-old child who sustained several injuries and contusions in Husan town west of Bethlehem.
Armed settlers shot and injured a Palestinian man near Asira al Qiblia village on July 14. He was moved the hospital.
The settlers from Yizhar attacked Hawara village wounding one Palestinian.
Land seizure: As reported on July 9 2019, Israeli settlers seized Palestinian-owned stores in Hebron city.
Israeli settlers assaulted a Palestinian minor (16) in central Hebron on Wednesday May 18, 2016. The minor was detained in the aftermath of the attack for attempting to defend himself.

On Saturday, May 14 some settlers stormed a Palestinian house in Tal Rumeida neighbourhood in the southern West Bank city of Hebron. A woman and her child were injured during the attack.
